tool_args.label: Automated Revenue Recovery System - TransCore LP
tool_args.raw_text: TBTA seeks approval to ratify a modification to a competitively negotiated personal service contract with TransCore, LP to implement an automated Revenue Recovery System.

payload.description: Implement an automated Revenue Recovery System to review images of obstructed and altered plates and determine owners by identifying missing license plate characters. Cost $3,394,170 includes liquidated damages and two years of support and maintenance.

evidence_refs.source_quote: TBTA seeks approval to ratify a modification to a competitively negotiated personal service contract with TransCore, LP to implement an automated Revenue Recovery System

TransCore, LP Eight years Contract # PSC-13-2949.17 TBTA is seeking Board approval to approve the ratification of a modification to the competitively negotiated personal service contract for All-Electronic Open-Road Tolling in order to add funding to implement an automated Revenue Recovery System. $3,394,170

tool_args.raw_text: TBTA also continues persistent toll violator and license plate enforcement at all its crossings, as well as in and around the Congestion Relief Zone.

payload.description: President Sheridan provided an update on toll evasion enforcement. Chair Mack underscored the importance of toll evasion enforcement.

evidence_refs.source_quote: TBTA also continues persistent toll violator and license plate enforcement at all its crossings, as well as in and around the Congestion Relief Zone

tool_args.label: TBTA budget savings from cost-reduction effort
tool_args.raw_text: These include adjusting budgets to align with historical spending and continued efficiencies from Open Road Tolling.

payload.claim_text: TBTA identified $30 million in annual recurring budget savings starting in 2025. These include adjusting budgets to align with historical spending and continued efficiencies from Open Road Tolling. Through June, TBTA is favorable for expenses and is on pace to meet its targets at the end of the year. These savings will translate into a larger surplus for mass transit in 2025.
payload.description: President Sheridan stated that as part of the broader MTA cost-reduction effort, TBTA identified $30 million in annual recurring budget savings starting in 2025.

evidence_refs.source_quote: President Sheridan stated that, as part of the broader MTA cost-reduction effort, TBTA identified $30 million in annual recurring budget savings starting in 2025
